content.full_text:
City Council

People
Pride
Progress

City Hall
P.O. Box 1440
23119 Cottonwood Ave.
Building B
Moreno Valley, CA 92388-9664

(714) 243-3008
FAX: (714) 243-3760

June 24, 1991

Mr. John Wallace, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Re: Conflict of Interest

Dear Mr. Wallace:

I serve as elected councilmember and Mayor Pro Tem of the City of Moreno Valley, California. My husband is employed as the City's Public Safety Coordinator. I would appreciate advice from the Commission regarding my duties under the Political Reform Act, especially possible conflicts of interest arising out of my specific situation.

The enclosed memorandum from Edward Duddy, City Attorney, dated June 7, 1991, will explain in more detail the particular circumstance which has prompted my request.

Should you have any questions or need further information regarding the matter, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Very truly yours,

Denise V. Lanning
Mayor Pro Tem, City of Moreno Valley

DVL:rh

cc:
Judith A. Nieburger, Mayor
A.F. Chelbana, Councilman
Cynthia A. Crothers, Councilwoman
Richard A. Stewart, Councilman
Norman R. King, City Manager
Edward Duddy, City Attorney


City Attorney's Office
3750 University Avenue
Suite 550
Riverside, CA 92501

People
Pride
Progress                                      (714) 784-3800
                                             FAX: (714) 781-4083


M E M O R A N D U M


CONFIDENTIAL

TO:        Denise Lanning, Mayor Pro Tem

FROM:      Edward Duddy, City Attorney (243-3033)

DATE:      June 7, 1991

SUBJECT:   Animal Control Program - Participation in Decisions

Denise:

     On June 4, 1991, you informed me that a question had been
raised as to whether or not the Political Reform Act and
regulations adopted by the Fair Political Practices Commission
("FPPC") prohibited your participation in City business
related to animal control, such as contracts, purchases and
ordinances. The question was raised because your spouse,
James Lanning, is employed by the City as Public Safety
Coordinator, the duties of which office include administration
of various aspects of the City's animal control program.

     On June 11, 1991, the City Council is scheduled to
consider a contract which, if approved, would employ a private
firm to provide animal control services which are presently
being furnished by the Riverside Humane Society.

     Mr. Lanning is a career employee of the City and neither
his compensation, tenure, or expectations of advancement or
demotion are dependent upon what entity provides animal
control services for the City. Neither you nor Mr. Lanning
have any financial or other interest in the current service
provider, in the proposed new provider, or in any provider of
animal control services, equipment or supplies.

     The above-referenced June 11th agenda item was first
scheduled for City Council consideration on May 14, 1991. At
that time the City Council expressed an intent to enter into a
contract with the new provider and instructed staff to return
to the Council with a written agreement. Prior to any
consideration of that matter (as well as earlier matters
related to animal control), you asked my advice as to whether

Page 2


you could participate in consideration of the matter.  Based
pretty much on the circumstances set forth above, I advised
you that you were not required to disqualify yourself from
participation in the matter.

     When you informed me that the question had been raised
again, as related above, I revisited the applicable FPPC
regulations and concluded, as before, that you were not
disqualified.  I so informed you, and recommended for further
protection that at least informal advice be sought from the
FPPC on the issue.  You authorized me to seek that advice.

     On June 6, 1991, FPPC Staff Attorney John Wallace
informed me by telephone that, based on the foregoing
circumstances as related above, there did not appear to be any
problem if you participated in the animal control item on the
June 11th City Council agenda.  Mr. Wallace stressed that,
pursuant to regular FPPC policy, such oral advice could apply
only to a prospective action and not to any past items; also,
Mr. Wallace pointed out that the advice would not have any
effect or bearing on any ongoing investigation which the FPPC
Enforcement Division may have undertaken.  Mr. Wallace
referred to 2CCR 18702.1 as the FPPC regulation in point.  A
copy of the regulation is attached for your information.

     One further point:  Mr. Wallace suggested that you might
find it helpful to ask for formal written advice from his
office which would provide guidelines for you as a City
councilmember married to an employee of the City.  Pursuant to
our telephone conversation of June 6th, I will prepare a draft
letter for your signature requesting such advice.

     Please let me know if there is anything further you wish
from me in respect to this matter

                                        E.D.

ED/mb

cc:  Mayor Nieburger
     Councilmember Chelbana
     Councilmember Crothers
     Councilmember Stewart
     City Manager
     James Lanning, Public Safety Coordinator
     John Wallace, Esq., FPPC


MVMEMO24
